pages.full_text:
__Friday 27th September__
2.30. Simon, Eve + Tinder set off to meet William + Joseph for camping trips. We sat on Haddenham + Thame platform + William + Joseph got off the train directly opposite us, William carrying a scooter + an enormous pack.
We camped at White Mark Farm, [illegible] near the Carriers Arms Pub, Watlington.
We put up the tent + had bacon sandwiches. William put Joseph in a fleece inside his new sleeping bag.
     In the 1700s the vicar was miffed because his church didn't have a spire. So he carved one out of the chalk on the hillside. When he sat in his vicarage, the spire appeared over his church - the White Mark.
__Saturday 28th__
Charles arrived [sticker] at 8.35. for breakfast
pages.route_sketch_description: A simple hand-drawn sketch of a church/spire shape on the right side of the page — appears to illustrate the story of the vicar carving a chalk spire shape on the hillside (the White Mark), showing a tall pointed spire above a rectangular base.
pages.notes: Year cannot be confirmed from page content alone. 'Friday 27th September' is consistent with 2013 or 2019; position in volume B (pdf_page 224 of 373) loosely suggests 2013. The word after 'White Mark Farm,' has asterisk-like marks (possibly a footnote marker or rating stars) and is otherwise [illegible]. A decorative printed sticker (wheat/grain motif) appears between 'arrived' and '8.35'. Tinder is likely a dog's name. 'White Mark' is the local chalk landmark near Watlington, Oxfordshire.

pages.full_text:
after taking Jenny to her training day in London. She is working 5 days a week + training on Saturdays. Charles stayed for breakfast, lunch + supper.
     We went on quite a walk in the morning. The ground was mostly too rough for the scooter. William carried J. on his shoulders + Charles did sometimes. Unfortunately Tinder got into a field + chased some cows. Joseph had red crocs on which kept falling off. We passed a pond, Willow Pond, which had melted away. We were partly on the Ridgeway. Back at Watlington we went to a craft sale in the Memorial Hall + then had lunch at the Carriers Arms.
     Then we all had a long nap at the van. After tea Simon + William walked up the hill to the White Mark. The weather had been good, a bit of sun. It rained in the night.
pages.notes: Continues from Saturday 28th on the left page. 'J.' is Joseph. 'Tinder' is the family dog. A decorative printed sticker (wheat/grain motif) appears at the bottom of the page. The Ridgeway is the ancient long-distance path through Oxfordshire. Willow Pond reference is notable — diarist says it had 'melted away', suggesting it had dried up.
